description: The Reform Front of Iran (Jebhe-ye Eslahaat-e Iran) is the successor coalition to the [Council for Coordinating the Reforms Front](/orgs/council-for-coordinating-reforms-front), established in 2020 as the overarching umbrella for Iran's reformist political camp. Originally called the Reformist Consensus-Building Body, it coordinates the strategy of reformist parties and figures. [Behzad Nabavi](/people/behzad-nabavi) chaired the front during its first two years before [Azar Mansouri](/people/azar-mansouri) succeeded him in July 2023. The coalition traces its roots to the groups that backed [Mohammad Khatami](/people/mohammad-khatami)'s 1997 presidential campaign and has since played a role in the elections of [Hassan Rouhani](/people/hassan-rouhani) and [Masoud Pezeshkian](/people/masoud-pezeshkian).
editorial_notes: Successor to Council for Coordinating the Reforms Front. Azar Mansouri became chair July 2023. Behzad Nabavi was first chair 2020-2022.
